LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE OCT-24-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. OFF CALENDAR. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO STAY THIS CASE PENDING THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT'S RESOLUTION OF RODRIGUEZ V. FCA US, LLC (2022) 77 CAL.APP.5TH 209 IS GRANTED (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-24-2025 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-20-2024 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-18-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar. Plaintiff's request to stay this case pending the California Supreme Court's resolution of Rodriguez v. FCA US, LLC (2022) 77 Cal.App.5th 209 is granted. (CCP 128(a)(8).) The main issue in this case is whether the express warranty provisions of the Song-Beverly Act apply to used vehicles. There is a split of authority regarding that issue and the California Supreme Court recently heard argument on the matter. This court anticipates that the split between Rodriguez and Stiles v. Kia Motors America, Inc. (2024) 101 Cal.App.5th 913, which is also under review, will be resolved shortly. Defendant may seek to lift the stay and calendar an updated summary judgment motion after the California Supreme Court issues its decision.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-13-2023 Set For Trial On Nov-25-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
